content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA consisting of NNN **distinct** positive integers.

In one operation, you can:


Choose a non-negative integer XXX;
For all (1≤i≤N)(1\le i\le N)(1≤i≤N), set Ai:=Ai⊕XA_i := A_i \oplus XAi​:=Ai​⊕X, where ⊕\oplus⊕ denotes the bitwise XOR operation.
Find the **maximum** possible length of the **longest strictly increasing subarray** after performing the given operation **exactly once**.

Note:


An array PPP is a subarray of an array QQQ if PPP can be obtained from QQQ by deletion of several (possibly zero or all) elements from the beginning and several (possibly zero or all) elements from the end.
The longest strictly increasing subarray of QQQ is the longest (having most elements) subarray of QQQ which is sorted in **strictly increasing** order.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input:

The first line of each test case contains NNN - the size of the array.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers, A1,A2,…ANA_1, A_2, \ldots A_NA1​,A2​,…AN​, the elements of the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the **maximum** possible length of the **longest strictly increasing subarray** after performing the given operation **exactly once**.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10610^6106.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Choose X=1X = 1X=1. Thus the elements of the array become [3⊕1,2⊕1,5⊕1]=[2,3,4][3\oplus 1, 2\oplus 1, 5\oplus 1] = [2, 3, 4][3⊕1,2⊕1,5⊕1]=[2,3,4]. Thus, the longest strictly increasing subarray is [2,3,4][2, 3, 4][2,3,4] having length 333.
Test case 222: Choose X=0X = 0X=0. Thus the elements of the array become [10⊕0,20⊕0,30⊕0,40⊕0]=[10,20,30,40][10\oplus 0, 20\oplus 0, 30\oplus 0, 40\oplus 0] = [10, 20, 30, 40][10⊕0,20⊕0,30⊕0,40⊕0]=[10,20,30,40]. Thus, the longest strictly increasing subarray is [10,20,30,40][10, 20, 30, 40][10,20,30,40] having length 444.
Test case 333: Choose X=15X = 15X=15. Thus the elements of the array become [14⊕15,11⊕15,9⊕15,3⊕15,7⊕15]=[1,4,6,12,8][14\oplus 15, 11\oplus 15, 9\oplus 15, 3\oplus 15, 7\oplus 15] = [1, 4, 6, 12, 8][14⊕15,11⊕15,9⊕15,3⊕15,7⊕15]=[1,4,6,12,8]. Thus, the longest strictly increasing subarray is [1,4,6,12][1, 4, 6, 12][1,4,6,12] having length 444.
